claim_status: Task approval template only; no task is approved, running, accepted, release-gated, production-authorized, source-strategy-approved, readiness-promoted, Chrome-class, performance, compatibility, security, accessibility, beta, stable, release, daily-driver, or production claim is created by this template.
required_approval_inputs: proposed queue task ID and current proposed task digest
owner scope and named approving owner
independent reviewer scope and named independent reviewer
requirements, risks, ADRs, PB gates, RQ links, and work packages touched
allowed paths, prohibited paths, generated-output rules, and source-tree cleanliness policy
granted tools, exact network allowlist, credential identifiers, and credential expiry
resource budget for wall time, CPU time, memory, disk, network, workers, and retries
evidence bundle destination, retention policy, artifact hash policy, and raw-output retention
rollback plan, rollback evidence, and rejected-task cleanup criteria
claim boundary preserving no readiness promotion and no public product claim
immutable_manifest_requirements: approved manifest status must be reviewed or ready before execution begins
approved manifest must include an immutable source queue digest and approval timestamp
approved manifest must include owner and independent reviewer identifiers that are not the implementing agent
approved manifest must bind every allowed path and prohibited path before tool execution
approved manifest must bind every granted network destination and credential identifier before execution
approved manifest must bind exact evidence bundle and agent run manifest locations before execution
approved manifest must preserve preconditions, acceptance criteria, negative tests, rollback, dependencies, and expiry
approved manifest must record unsupported behavior and residual risk next to positive evidence
approved manifest must not weaken the proposed queue task, dependency graph, or pre-build readiness gates
authority_controls: deny all paths except approved allowed paths
deny all network except approved exact destinations
deny production signing keys, offline roots, unrestricted cloud credentials, real user profiles, and production browser profiles
deny source-strategy adoption, readiness promotion, release promotion, and public claims as agent actions
deny self-approval, self-merge, self-acceptance, and self-review
bound wall time, CPU, memory, disk, network, worker count, and retry count
record every command, tool call, generated diff, generated artifact, failure, retry, override, and escalation
preserve source-tree cleanliness and keep build caches or generated outputs outside source unless explicitly approved
escalate authority outside the manifest instead of expanding scope during execution
require owner review before any task can move from review_pending to accepted
evidence_bundle_requirements: source commit, branch, instruction digest, toolchain digest, environment digest, and manifest digest
raw command output, exit codes, test logs, validation output, and artifact SHA-256 hashes
negative-test, failure, timeout, retry, cancellation, rollback, and escalation records
review decision from an independent reviewer with accepted, rejected, or rolled-back disposition
updated affected Blueprint chapters, detailed books, readiness records, risks, requirements, task queue, and validators
explicit unsupported behavior and residual risk after the task
rejection_rules: reject a proposed queue row used as execution approval
reject an approval manifest without named owner and named independent reviewer
reject owner and independent reviewer being the same person or the implementing agent
reject missing allowed path, prohibited path, network, credential, budget, evidence bundle, rollback, dependency, or expiry controls
reject authority expansion after execution begins without a new owner-approved manifest
reject missing raw artifacts, hashes, exit codes, validation output, source-tree status, or failure accounting
reject any task that weakens sandbox, site isolation, certificate validation, update verification, permission policy, or no-claim boundaries to pass
reject source-strategy adoption, production signing, release promotion, stable support, or public browser claim from task execution
reject any accepted status without independent review after the latest change
